page: Amazon Web Services g6.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 8 vCPUs (4 physical cores) running at 3.4 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance includes a dedicated NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM, alongside 450 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests and strong text processing in Geekbench, both ranking in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores fall in the average range. Notably, the instance achieves top-tier performance in the top 10% for LLM inference speeds during both prompt processing and text generation using small (135M) and medium (7B) models.

A primary hardware tradeoff is memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks (256MB), where both read and write speeds rank in the weak category (bottom 25%). However, cached small-block read bandwidth remains average. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the inclusion of a dedicated 22 GB VRAM GPU and local NVMe storage on a mid-tier 8-vCPU footprint provides a dense resource profile for accelerated workloads without requiring larger, more expensive instance classes.

This instance is configured for graphics-intensive applications, machine learning inference (specifically small to medium LLM deployment), video transcoding, and database operations that benefit from local NVMe SSD storage.
description: Amazon Web Services g6.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 8 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a dedicated NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM. It includes 450 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 5 Gbps baseline network speed. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU execution and top-tier LLM inference speeds for small and medium models, though large-block memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%. Qualitatively, the g6.2xlarge offers a cost-efficient entry point for hardware-accelerated workloads by bundling dedicated GPU resources and fast local storage within a moderate 8-vCPU envelope. This profile is suited for machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, a 22 GB NVIDIA L4 GPU, and 450 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier LLM inference performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6.2xlarge is an 8-vCPU GPU instance with an NVIDIA L4 GPU, 32 GB RAM, and 450 GB NVMe SSD storage for accelerated workloads.
tagline: An x86 virtual server featuring a dedicated NVIDIA L4 GPU, local NVMe storage, and top-tier small-to-medium LLM inference performance.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7R13 CPU with 8 vCPUs and 32 GB DDR4 RAM
NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU with 22 GB VRAM
450 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier benchmark results for small and medium LLM inference
Weak memory bandwidth performance for large uncached blocks
Suited for machine learning inference, graphics processing, and databases
